hooks.command: cmd=$(echo $TOOL_INPUT | jq -r '.command // empty'); if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE '(scripts/train\.py|scripts/eval\.py|scripts/run_sweep\.py|scripts/generate_)' && [ ! -f '.epm-authorized' ]; then echo 'BLOCKED: Experiment commands must run through /issue. Create a task first: `uv run python scripts/task.py new --kind experiment --title "..."`, then use /issue <N> where N is the printed task number.' >&2; exit 2; fi

cmd=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE 'ssh +epm-issue-[0-9]+ +.*git +pull'; then if [ -n "$(cd $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR && git status --porcelain 2>/dev/null)" ]; then echo 'WARNING: ssh + git pull on a pod with dirty local tree. Local has uncommitted changes — pulling on the pod creates divergent states. Commit/stash locally before pulling on the pod.' >&2; fi; fi

file_path=$(jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty'); if [ -n "$file_path" ] && echo "$file_path" | grep -qE '^/workspace/'; then echo "WARNING: Editing $file_path under /workspace/. CLAUDE.md: 'All code changes on local VM, never on pods.' Likely a stale symlink or misconfigured clone — fix the path." >&2; fi

a=$(printf '%s' "${EPM_AUTONOMOUS_SESSION:-}" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]'); if [ -n "$a" ] && [ "$a" != "0" ] && [ "$a" != "false" ] && [ "$a" != "no" ]; then echo 'BLOCKED (autonomous session): AskUserQuestion is forbidden when EPM_AUTONOMOUS_SESSION is set. Autonomous policy: asking IS the failure mode — every fork must auto-resolve toward the task Goal. The conditional gates that previously asked (plan_approval, whack_a_mole_pivot, compute_deviation_resolution, concern_deferral_request, experiment_goal_refine, fact_candidates, living_docs_update, etc.) all have auto-resolve rules in `.claude/skills/issue/SKILL.md` § Autonomous session behavior. plan_approval is code-enforced by `task.py set-status <N> plan_pending --auto-approve-if-autonomous --gpu-hours <X>`. If you genuinely need user input that no autonomous reasoning can supply, post `epm:failure v1` with `failure_class: <code|infra|data>` and `set-status <N> blocked` instead. Re-read the task status and act on it; do NOT ask the user.' >&2; exit 2; fi

file_path=$(jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // .tool_response.filePath // empty'); if [ -n "$file_path" ] && echo "$file_path" | grep -qE '\.py$'; then cd $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR && uv run ruff check --fix --quiet "$file_path" 2>/dev/null; uv run ruff format --quiet "$file_path" 2>/dev/null; fi
